epic.description: Epic covering all 14 missing platform features identified in gap analysis. Each feature follows SPARC methodology with clear DONE criteria.

tickets.summary: Docker + docker-compose for full platform stack
tickets.description: As a developer, I want to run the entire ProjectZero stack locally with one command so that I can develop and test without manual service setup.

sparc.specification: Create Dockerfile for backend (FastAPI), frontend (React/Vite), temporal worker. Create docker-compose.yml orchestrating: postgres, redis, temporal-server, temporal-ui, backend, frontend, temporal-worker. Include volume mounts for hot-reload. Include health checks.
sparc.pseudocode: docker-compose.yml: postgres:15 → redis:7 → temporal-server (depends: postgres) → temporal-ui (depends: temporal-server) → backend (depends: postgres, redis, temporal) → frontend (depends: backend) → worker (depends: temporal, backend)
sparc.architecture: Multi-container Docker Compose. Postgres on 5432, Redis on 6379, Temporal on 7233, Temporal UI on 8233, Backend on 8000, Frontend on 3000. Shared network. Named volumes for data persistence.
sparc.realization: Write Dockerfiles with multi-stage builds. Write docker-compose.yml. Test all services start and communicate. Backend runs migrations on startup.
sparc.completion: All services start with `docker-compose up`. Health checks pass. Frontend loads. Backend responds. Temporal UI accessible.

acceptance_criteria: `docker-compose up` starts all 7 services without errors
Backend health check passes at localhost:8000/health
Postgres, Redis reachable from backend container
Hot-reload works for backend and frontend in dev mode
tickets.done_state: All 7 containers running. Health checks green. Frontend renders dashboard. Backend serves API. Temporal processes test workflow. No manual setup steps required beyond `docker-compose up`.

tickets.summary: Alembic database migrations for all models
tickets.description: As a developer, I want database schema managed through versioned migrations so that schema changes are repeatable, reversible, and tracked.

sparc.specification: Initialize Alembic in platform/backend/. Generate initial migration for all 14 models (WorkflowRun, WorkflowStep, WorkflowApproval, WorkflowArtifact, WorkflowAudit, WorkflowTrigger, AgentContribution, UserActivity, UserSession, SystemEvent, Memory, Decision, Pattern, Conversation). Configure for async SQLAlchemy.
sparc.pseudocode: alembic init → configure env.py for async → autogenerate migration → test upgrade → test downgrade
sparc.architecture: Alembic migrations in platform/backend/migrations/versions/. env.py configured for async PostgreSQL. All models imported in env.py target_metadata.
sparc.realization: Run alembic init. Edit env.py for async. Generate first migration. Test up/down.
sparc.completion: `alembic upgrade head` creates all tables. `alembic downgrade -1` reverses cleanly.

acceptance_criteria: Initial migration creates all 14 model tables
`alembic upgrade head` succeeds on clean database
All indexes and constraints match model definitions
tickets.done_state: Running `alembic upgrade head` on empty Postgres creates complete schema matching all 14 SQLAlchemy models. Downgrade works. Migration embedded in Docker startup.

tickets.description: As a platform user, I want to authenticate and have my actions tracked to my identity so that all activity is attributable and access is controlled.

sparc.specification: User model (id, email, name, role, password_hash, is_active). JWT auth (access token 15min, refresh token 7d). Roles: admin, developer, viewer. Routes: POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, GET /auth/me. Password hashing with bcrypt. Auth middleware on all /api/v1/ routes. CORS token handling.
sparc.pseudocode: register(email, password, name) → hash pw → save user → return tokens. login(email, password) → verify → issue JWT. middleware: extract token → verify → inject user into request.
sparc.architecture: User model in models/user.py. Auth service in services/auth_service.py. Auth routes in api/routes/auth.py. JWT middleware in core/auth.py. Dependencies inject current_user.
sparc.realization: Implement User model, auth service (register, login, verify, refresh), JWT utility, auth dependency, routes. Add auth middleware to existing routes.
sparc.completion: Register, login, refresh work. Protected routes require valid JWT. Activity tracking includes user_id from JWT.

acceptance_criteria: Login returns JWT access + refresh tokens
Protected routes reject requests without valid token
User roles enforced (admin can manage users, developer can execute, viewer read-only)
Password stored as bcrypt hash, never plaintext
Activity tracking auto-captures user_id from JWT
tickets.done_state: User registers → logs in → gets JWT → accesses protected routes → activity logged with user identity. Invalid/expired tokens rejected with 401. Admin can list/deactivate users.

tickets.summary: Redis client service for caching and pub/sub
tickets.description: As the platform, I need Redis for caching frequently-accessed data and real-time pub/sub for live UI updates.

sparc.specification: Redis client in core/redis.py using redis.asyncio. Cache service: get/set/delete with TTL. Pub/sub: publish workflow state changes, subscribe from WebSocket handler. Cache targets: dashboard summary (30s TTL), integration status (60s TTL), agent status (10s TTL).
sparc.pseudocode: init: redis.from_url(REDIS_URL). cache_get(key) → redis.get. cache_set(key, value, ttl) → redis.setex. publish(channel, message) → redis.publish. subscribe(channel) → redis.subscribe + async iterator.
sparc.architecture: core/redis.py (connection factory), services/cache_service.py (cache operations), used by routes for response caching.
sparc.realization: Implement redis client, cache service, add caching to dashboard and agent status routes.
sparc.completion: Redis connected. Dashboard caches. Pub/sub publishes workflow events.

acceptance_criteria: Cache invalidated on workflow state change
tickets.done_state: Redis connected. GET /dashboard/summary serves from cache on repeat calls. Workflow state change invalidates cache. Redis PING returns PONG in health check.

tickets.summary: GitHub API client for repo and PR management
tickets.description: As the platform, I need to create GitHub repos, branches, and PRs programmatically during product bootstrap and feature development.

sparc.specification: GitHub service using httpx. Operations: create_repo(org, name), create_branch(repo, branch, from_branch), create_pr(repo, title, body, head, base), get_repo(org, name), list_prs(repo), merge_pr(repo, pr_number). Auth via GITHUB_TOKEN. Error handling with retry.
sparc.pseudocode: create_repo: POST /orgs/{org}/repos → return repo_url. create_pr: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls → return pr_url. All calls: headers={Authorization: token {GITHUB_TOKEN}}.
sparc.architecture: services/github_service.py (async httpx client). Used by /bootstrap-product command and Temporal activities.
sparc.realization: Implement all operations. Add to bootstrap flow. Test with real API.
sparc.completion: /bootstrap-product creates real GitHub repo. Feature workflow creates branch + PR.

acceptance_criteria: Create PR with title, description, base branch
tickets.done_state: /bootstrap-product creates GitHub repo. /implement creates feature branch. PR created after implementation. GitHub integration validates in /factory-init.

tickets.summary: JIRA API client for ticket lifecycle management
tickets.description: As the platform, I need to create and manage JIRA tickets automatically as features move through workflow stages.

sparc.specification: JIRA service using httpx + Basic Auth. Operations: create_project(key, name), create_issue(project, type, summary, description, priority, labels), update_issue(key, fields), transition_issue(key, transition_id), get_issue(key), search_issues(jql), add_comment(key, comment), create_sprint(board_id, name). Auth: email:token base64.
sparc.pseudocode: create_issue: POST /rest/api/3/issue → return issue_key. transition: POST /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/transitions. All calls: auth=(email, token).
sparc.architecture: services/jira_service.py. Temporal activity calls JIRA service at each stage transition. Maps workflow stages to JIRA statuses.
sparc.realization: Implement all operations. Wire into Temporal record_step activity. Auto-create tickets during /spec. Auto-transition during workflow execution.
sparc.completion: /spec creates JIRA tickets. Workflow transitions update ticket status. Comments added at each stage.

acceptance_criteria: Create tickets with summary, description, acceptance criteria, story points
Transition tickets as workflow progresses (To Do → In Progress → In Review → Done)
Add comments at each stage with agent output summary
Handle rate limits and auth errors gracefully
tickets.done_state: Running /spec creates JIRA tickets. Running /implement transitions tickets through statuses. Each stage adds comment. Ticket reaches 'Done' when workflow completes.

tickets.summary: Confluence API client for documentation hub management
tickets.description: As the platform, I need to create and update Confluence spaces and pages to maintain living project documentation.

sparc.specification: Confluence service using httpx. Operations: create_space(key, name), create_page(space, title, body, parent_id), update_page(page_id, title, body, version), get_page(page_id), search(cql). Create hub structure from templates during bootstrap.
sparc.pseudocode: create_space: POST /rest/api/space. create_page: POST /rest/api/content with storage format body. update: PUT /rest/api/content/{id} with version increment.
sparc.architecture: services/confluence_service.py. Used by /bootstrap-product (create hub) and phase transitions (update pages).
sparc.realization: Implement operations. Create hub structure from .claude/templates/confluence-*. Update pages at phase boundaries.
sparc.completion: /bootstrap-product creates Confluence space with hub. Phase completions update relevant pages.

acceptance_criteria: Create hub pages from templates (overview, BMAD, architecture, modules, etc.)
Update pages at phase boundaries with generated content
tickets.done_state: /bootstrap-product creates Confluence space with 10 hub pages. /arch updates architecture page. /release updates release notes page. All from templates.

tickets.summary: AI/Claude execution service for agent work
tickets.description: As an AI agent, I need to call Claude API to perform actual work (write specs, generate code, review, etc.) within Temporal workflow steps.

sparc.specification: AI service wrapping Anthropic SDK. Operations: execute_agent(agent_type, prompt, context, max_tokens), stream_agent(agent_type, prompt, context). Agent context loading: read Brain memories + relevant .claude/agents/ definition + skill instructions. Response parsing: extract code blocks, decisions, artifacts. Token tracking per execution.
sparc.pseudocode: execute_agent: load_context(agent_type, product_id) → build_prompt(agent_def + memories + task) → anthropic.messages.create() → parse_response() → store_artifacts() → write_brain()
sparc.architecture: services/ai_service.py (Anthropic client wrapper). Used by Temporal execute_stage activity. Each agent type maps to system prompt from .claude/agents/. Context enriched from Brain.
sparc.realization: Implement Anthropic client, agent context builder, prompt templating, response parser, artifact extractor. Wire into execute_stage activity.
sparc.completion: Temporal execute_stage calls Claude API with agent-specific context. Response parsed into artifacts. Brain updated.

acceptance_criteria: Claude API called with agent-specific system prompt
Brain memories loaded as context before each call
Response parsed: code blocks extracted, decisions captured
Artifacts stored in DB and optionally in MinIO
Token usage tracked per agent execution
Handles API errors, rate limits, context overflow gracefully
tickets.done_state: Temporal workflow step calls execute_stage → AI service loads agent context from Brain + .claude/agents/ → calls Claude API → parses response → stores artifacts → writes learnings to Brain. Token usage logged.

tickets.summary: MinIO object storage for document and artifact uploads
tickets.description: As the platform, I need object storage for uploaded documents (PRDs, BMADs) and generated artifacts (code, reports, diagrams).

sparc.specification: MinIO service using minio-py SDK. Operations: upload_file(bucket, key, file), download_file(bucket, key), get_presigned_url(bucket, key, expiry), list_objects(bucket, prefix), delete_object(bucket, key). Buckets: documents (user uploads), artifacts (generated), exports (reports).
sparc.pseudocode: upload: client.put_object(bucket, key, data, length). download: client.get_object(bucket, key). presigned: client.presigned_get_object(bucket, key, expires=3600).
sparc.architecture: services/storage_service.py. MinIO in docker-compose. Used by: /vision-to-prd (store uploaded PRD), artifact generation (store outputs), /release (store release packages).
sparc.realization: Implement MinIO client, storage service, upload route. Add MinIO to docker-compose. Wire into artifact workflow.
sparc.completion: Files upload/download via API. Artifacts stored in MinIO. Presigned URLs for frontend display.

tickets.done_state: User uploads PRD → stored in MinIO. Workflow generates artifact → stored in MinIO. Frontend displays via presigned URL. All buckets created on startup.

tickets.summary: WebSocket real-time updates for Control Tower
tickets.description: As a developer watching the Control Tower, I want real-time updates without refreshing so that I see workflow progress, agent activity, and approvals live.

sparc.specification: WebSocket endpoint at /ws. Channels: workflow_updates (stage transitions), agent_activity (agent start/complete), approvals (new pending), system_events. Backend publishes via Redis pub/sub → WebSocket broadcaster. Frontend: useWebSocket hook, auto-reconnect.
sparc.pseudocode: backend: @app.websocket('/ws') → subscribe redis channels → forward to client. frontend: useWebSocket('/ws') → onMessage → update React Query cache.
sparc.architecture: WebSocket handler in backend using Starlette. Redis pub/sub for cross-process broadcasting. Frontend hook updates React Query cache for instant UI refresh.
sparc.realization: Implement WS handler, Redis subscriber, message router. Frontend hook with reconnection. Update Dashboard, DevMonitor, Approvals pages.
sparc.completion: Workflow state change → Redis pub → WebSocket → UI updates without refresh.

acceptance_criteria: Workflow stage transitions appear in real-time
tickets.done_state: Start workflow → Dashboard updates in real-time. Approval requested → Approvals page shows instantly. Agent starts → DevMonitor shows live. No polling required.

tickets.description: As an operator, I want metrics, error tracking, and distributed tracing so that I can monitor platform health and debug issues.

sparc.specification: Prometheus metrics endpoint at /metrics (request count, latency histogram, active workflows, agent executions). Sentry integration for error tracking. OpenTelemetry tracing for distributed request tracing across FastAPI → Temporal → agents. Grafana dashboard.
sparc.pseudocode: prometheus: instrument fastapi with prometheus_fastapi_instrumentator. sentry: sentry_sdk.init(dsn). otel: configure TracerProvider with OTLP exporter.
sparc.architecture: core/observability.py (init all providers). Prometheus at /metrics. Sentry error handler. OTEL trace context propagated to Temporal activities.
sparc.realization: Add prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator, sentry-sdk, opentelemetry packages. Configure in app startup. Add Prometheus + Grafana to docker-compose.
sparc.completion: /metrics returns Prometheus data. Errors appear in Sentry. Traces span FastAPI → Temporal.

acceptance_criteria: Grafana dashboard shows request rate, latency, error rate
Errors auto-reported to Sentry with context
Distributed traces span API → Temporal → activities
tickets.done_state: Grafana dashboard shows platform health. Sentry captures errors with stack traces. Traces visible in Jaeger/OTEL collector. Custom workflow metrics tracked.

tickets.summary: Backend test suite: unit + integration + API tests
tickets.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive backend tests so that changes don't break existing functionality.

sparc.specification: pytest + pytest-asyncio + httpx for async tests. Unit tests: all services (workflow, brain, activity, auth). Integration tests: all API routes with test DB. Fixtures: test database, test user, sample workflow. Coverage target: 80%.
sparc.pseudocode: conftest.py: create test DB, async session, test client. test_workflows.py: test start, signal, query. test_brain.py: test memory CRUD, promotion. test_auth.py: test register, login, protected routes.
sparc.architecture: platform/backend/tests/ with conftest.py, test_*.py per module. Uses pytest-asyncio for async. Test DB created/dropped per session.
sparc.realization: Write conftest with fixtures. Write tests per service and route module. Run with coverage.

acceptance_criteria: Tests for all API routes (success + error cases)
Test database isolation (no pollution between tests)
tickets.done_state: `pytest` passes all tests. `pytest --cov` shows ≥ 80% coverage. Tests run in Docker. No flaky tests.

tickets.summary: Frontend test suite: component + integration tests
tickets.description: As a developer, I want frontend tests for all pages and components so that UI changes don't break functionality.

sparc.specification: Vitest + React Testing Library. Component tests: StatusBadge, StagePipeline. Page tests: Dashboard (renders cards), AgentsList (renders teams), Approvals (approve/reject). Mock API responses.
sparc.architecture: platform/frontend/src/__tests__/ or co-located *.test.tsx. Vitest config in vite.config.ts. MSW for API mocking.
sparc.realization: Configure Vitest. Write tests for all 13 pages and 2 components. Mock API with MSW.
sparc.completion: vitest passes. All pages render without errors. Key interactions tested.

acceptance_criteria: Tests for all 13 pages (render + key interactions)
Tests for StatusBadge and StagePipeline components
tickets.done_state: `npm test` passes all tests. All pages render. Approve/reject buttons work in tests. Mock API responses match real schema.

tickets.summary: Bootstrap product script with real API calls
tickets.description: As a user running /bootstrap-product, I want the system to actually create GitHub repo, JIRA project, and Confluence space via APIs, not just print instructions.

sparc.specification: Rewrite scripts/bootstrap-product.sh to call FastAPI endpoints which in turn call GitHub/JIRA/Confluence APIs. Flow: validate integrations → create GitHub repo → create JIRA project → create Confluence space + hub → copy product skeleton → init git → push. All via backend API.
sparc.pseudocode: curl POST /api/v1/integrations/validate → check all pass. curl POST /api/v1/github/repos → create repo. curl POST /api/v1/jira/projects → create project. curl POST /api/v1/confluence/spaces → create space.
sparc.architecture: Backend routes: POST /api/v1/bootstrap/product. Orchestrates GitHub + JIRA + Confluence service calls. Returns product config. Script calls this single endpoint.
sparc.realization: Create bootstrap route. Wire GitHub, JIRA, Confluence services. Update script to call backend. Add to Temporal as bootstrap workflow.
sparc.completion: /bootstrap-product creates real external resources. Product repo exists on GitHub. JIRA project exists. Confluence hub exists.

acceptance_criteria: Single API call creates GitHub repo + JIRA project + Confluence space
Script validates integrations before creating anything
Rollback on partial failure (delete created resources)
Git initialized and pushed to new repo
Confluence hub pages created from templates
tickets.done_state: Run /bootstrap-product → GitHub repo created and visible → JIRA project created with board → Confluence space created with 10 hub pages → product repo cloned locally with .claude OS → ready for /spec.
